fixes.reason: Cluster A (T004, T015, T072) showed ratio ~0.02 indicating cascade collapse on fea4 KO: the BA1/RA1/RA3 branch loses its FEA4 activation and Axillary_Meristem drops below the IM-size expansion gain. A direct FEA4 -> KRN (-1) shortcut re-routes the phenotypic dominance back to IM/row-number restriction.
fixes.biological_justification: Pautler et al. (Plant Cell 2015, DOI 10.1105/tpc.114.132506) report fea4 ears with enlarged IM and increased kernel row number; the row-number gain exceeds what IM-size expansion alone predicts, implicating FEA4 as a direct row-number restrictor. Edge sign -1 reflects that loss of FEA4 increases KRN.

fixes.description: T053 Bif1_het: BIF1 gene_modifier changed from 0.5 to 2.0
fixes.reason: Predicted direction was inverted (increased) with expected decreased. Root cause: Bif1 is a semidominant GOF stabilised Aux/IAA allele, not a knockdown. Encoding with gm=0.5 (KD) inverted the repressor effect.
fixes.biological_justification: Galli et al. (PNAS 2015, DOI 10.1073/pnas.1516473112) characterize Bif1 and Bif4 as semidominant point mutations in domain II of maize Aux/IAA proteins that stabilise the repressor against TIR1-mediated degradation, producing enhanced repressor activity.

fixes.description: T055 Bif1;Bif4 double: BIF1 gene_modifier changed from 0.0 to 2.0 (Bif4 partner not in network; partial mapping)
fixes.reason: Same semidominant GOF encoding issue as T053, compounded by the Bif4 component lacking a network node.
fixes.biological_justification: Galli et al. (PNAS 2015, DOI 10.1073/pnas.1516473112) — stabilised Aux/IAA GOF.
